## Imports

## Reference Link Extraction

Functions for handling markdown reference-style links like `[id]: url`.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/s01st/nbdev-mcp/blob/main/nbdev_mcp/utils/md.py#L21"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### extract_references

>  extract_references (lines:List[str])

*Extract reference-style link definitions from markdown lines.*

Reference links have the format `[id]: url`.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 9%" />
<col style="width: 38%" />
<col style="width: 52%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>lines</td>
<td>List</td>
<td>Lines of markdown text.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>Tuple</strong></td>
<td><strong>A tuple of (text_lines, ref_defs) where text_lines has
reference<br>definitions removed and ref_defs maps reference IDs to
their<br>full definition lines.</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/s01st/nbdev-mcp/blob/main/nbdev_mcp/utils/md.py#L60"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### refs_used

>  refs_used (text:str, ref_defs:Dict[str,str])

*Find which reference-style link IDs are used in the given text.*

Matches patterns like `[text][id]` or bare `[id]` references.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 9%" />
<col style="width: 38%" />
<col style="width: 52%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>text</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>Markdown text to search.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>ref_defs</td>
<td>Dict</td>
<td>Dictionary of known reference IDs to their definitions.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>List</strong></td>
<td><strong>List of reference IDs that appear in the text.</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

### clear_section_buffer

>  clear_section_buffer (cell_strs:List[str], buffer:List[str])

*Flush the buffer into cell_strs and clear it.*

Helper for grouping markdown content into logical sections.

target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### group_lines_by_sections

>  group_lines_by_sections (lines:List[str])

*Group markdown lines by heading sections.*

Each heading (`#`, `##`, etc.) becomes its own section, and subsequent
non-heading lines are grouped until the next heading.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 9%" />
<col style="width: 38%" />
<col style="width: 52%" />
</colgroup>

<tr>

<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>lines</td>
<td>List</td>
<td>Lines of markdown text.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>List</strong></td>
<td><strong>List of section strings, each representing a heading or
content block.</strong></td>
</tr>

## Cell Generation

Functions for generating notebook cells from markdown.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/s01st/nbdev-mcp/blob/main/nbdev_mcp/utils/md.py#L152"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### readd_reference_links

>  readd_reference_links (cells_raw:List[str], ref_defs:Dict[str,str])

*Append reference link definitions to cells that use them.*

Since reference style links might be used across a large markdown cell,
this is useful when splitting large markdown cells into sections for
code folding.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 9%" />
<col style="width: 38%" />
<col style="width: 52%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>cells_raw</td>
<td>List</td>
<td>Raw cell text strings.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>ref_defs</td>
<td>Dict</td>
<td>Reference definitions mapping IDs to full definition lines.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>List</strong></td>
<td><strong>List of notebook cell dictionaries with cell_type and
source.</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/s01st/nbdev-mcp/blob/main/nbdev_mcp/utils/md.py#L190"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### split_markdown_into_cells

>  split_markdown_into_cells (markdown:str)

*Split a markdown document into notebook-ready markdown cells.*

Rules:

- Each heading line (`#`, `##`, …) is its own cell
- Text after a heading up to the next heading is a separate cell
- Reference link definitions (`[id]: url`) are propagated to every cell
  that uses that id

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 9%" />
<col style="width: 38%" />
<col style="width: 52%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>markdown</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>Full markdown document text.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>List</strong></td>
<td><strong>List of notebook cell dictionaries ready to be added to a
notebook.</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
